title: Cultural Heritage Programs * Native American Community - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Cultural Heritage Programs * Native American Community 4 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF LANE EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT CHIFIN NATIVE YOUTH CENTER INDIAN EDUCATION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers learning, leadership, and cultural activities that engage Native youth and support a positive cultural identity. Provides culturally appropriate educational support to Native students, including tutoring and credit recovery, and supports Native families’ involvement in students’ education. 1084 G Street Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: Native American and Alaskan Native students and their families Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm (541) 726-4301 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NATIVE AMERICAN REHABILITATION ASSOCIATION OF THE NORTHWEST NARA NW CHILD AND FAMILY SERVICES GRESHAM NARA NW YOUTH PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides life skills training, methamphetamine and suicide prevention courses, cultural groups and activities. Can also provide referrals to other agency services. 620 NE 2nd Street Gresham, OR 97030 Eligibility: Ages 13-24 who identify as Native American Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 953-6598 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NATIVE AMERICAN REHABILITATION ASSOCIATION OF THE NORTHWEST NARA NW OUTPATIENT TREATMENT CENTER OUTPATIENT ADDICTION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides mental assessments and evaluation, substance abuce counseling, parenting groups, gambling addiction treatment, DUI rehiabiliation programs, and traditional ceremonies. 1631 SW Columbia Street Portland, OR 97201 Eligibility: Age 18 and older. Provides culturally appropriate services for the Native American community. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 307-7775 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NATIVE AMERICAN YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER CULTURAL ARTS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers weekly Culture Nights that provide dinner, beading, singing, crafting, intertribal drumming and dancing. 5135 NE Columbia Boulevard Portland, OR 97218 Eligibility: Primarily serves self-identified Native Americans of all ages Hours: -Wednesday 6pm-8pm during the school year. Wednesday holidays and school closures are observed. -Dinner: Wednesday 6:pm-6:30pm Email (503) 288-8177 267 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LANE EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT INDIAN EDUCATION PROGRAM NATIVE AMERICAN REHABILITATION ASSOCIATION OF THE NORTHWEST NARA NW YOUTH PROGRAM NATIVE AMERICAN REHABILITATION ASSOCIATION OF THE NORTHWEST OUTPATIENT ADDICTION SERVICES NATIVE AMERICAN YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER CULTURAL ARTS
